atoms.semantic_claim: ChainSpec stores a per-chain optional header extra_data size limit, defaulting Ethereum networks to MAXIMUM_EXTRA_DATA_SIZE.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Construct an Ethereum ChainSpec and a Summit genesis ChainSpec and assert Ethereum uses Some(MAXIMUM_EXTRA_DATA_SIZE) while Summit-style genesis specs use None.
atoms.required_change: Restore or replace ChainSpec-level storage and builder support for per-chain max_extra_data_bytes.
atoms.notes: The v2.2 ChainSpec struct does not contain this field.

atoms.semantic_claim: Genesis-derived deposit contracts use the Summit deposit event topic instead of the Ethereum mainnet deposit topic.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Load a genesis with depositContractAddress and assert ChainSpec.deposit_contract.topic equals SUMMIT_DEPOSIT_EVENT_TOPIC.
atoms.required_change: Restore or intentionally replace Summit deposit topic selection for genesis-derived deposit contracts.
atoms.notes: The v2.2 port still uses MAINNET_DEPOSIT_CONTRACT.topic when parsing a genesis deposit contract address.

atoms.semantic_claim: Chain specs built from genesis files disable the Ethereum extra_data size cap so Ritual/Summit chains can embed CL snapshots for recovery.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Validate a block header with extra_data longer than Ethereum's MAXIMUM_EXTRA_DATA_SIZE on a genesis-derived Ritual chain spec and assert it is not rejected solely for extra_data length.
atoms.required_change: Restore or explicitly reject the old Ritual/Summit unbounded extra_data policy for genesis-derived chain specs.
atoms.notes: No genesis-derived max_extra_data_bytes override was found in the v2.2 port.

atoms.semantic_claim: ChainSpec stores an optional post-merge validator/proposer base block reward parsed from genesis and builder configuration.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Deserialize a genesis containing baseBlockReward and assert ChainSpec preserves the reward value.
atoms.required_change: Restore or replace ChainSpec/base builder support for custom base_block_reward.
atoms.notes: The v2.2 ChainSpec struct and builder do not contain base_block_reward.

atoms.source_snippet: pub fn effective_block_reward(&self, block_number: u64) -> Option<u128>
atoms.semantic_claim: ChainSpec computes effective block rewards: standard pre-merge rewards and configured custom post-merge rewards, and exposes the custom reward via EthExecutorSpec.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Build a post-merge ChainSpec with a custom base block reward and assert execution/block reward calculation uses that reward rather than standard Ethereum no-reward behavior.
atoms.required_change: Determine whether v2.2's alloy-evm block reward helpers are enough, or restore ChainSpec/EthExecutorSpec custom base reward plumbing.
atoms.notes: The v2.2 port imports alloy_evm block reward helpers in tests, but no ChainSpec custom post-merge reward plumbing was found.

summary: Ritual chain-spec knobs for extra_data policy, Summit deposit topics, and custom block rewards are missing from the v2.2 port.
notes: Formatting-only changes in this file were ignored; only consensus/config behavior was atomized.
